from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about astronauts (or related entities) into a
    single fluent sentence. Uses predicate‑specific phrasing when possible
    and combines all clauses into one natural‑language sentence with relative clauses.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language templates.
    # The placeholder {subject} and {object} will be substituted.
    templates = {
        "almaMater": "{subject} studied at {object}",
        "birthDate": "{subject} was born on {object}",
        "birthName": "{subject}'s birth name was {object}",
        "birthPlace": "{subject} was born in {object}",
        "dateOfRetirement": "{subject} retired in {object}",
        "occupation": "{subject} worked as a {object}",
        "status": "{subject} is currently {object}",
        "timeInSpace": "{subject} spent {object} minutes in space",
        "mission": "{subject} flew on mission {object}",
        "selectedByNasa": "{subject} was selected by NASA in {object}",
        "deathDate": "{subject} died on {object}",
        "nationality": "{subject} was a citizen of {object}",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "{subject} served as chief of the astronaut office in {object}",
        "title": "{subject} held the title of {object}",
        "ribbonAward": "{subject} received the {object}",
        "operator": "{object} operated {subject}",
        "backupPilot": "{subject} had {object} as backup pilot",
        "commander": "{subject} was commanded by {object}",
        "crewMembers": "{subject} had {object} as a crew member",
        "representative": "{subject} was represented by {object}",
        "alternativeName": "{subject} is also known as {object}",
        "awards": "{subject} received {object} awards",
        "award": "{subject} received the {object}",
        "deathPlace": "{subject} died in {object}",
        "fossil": "{subject} is known for the fossil {object}",
        "senators": "{subject} is represented by senator {object}",
        "part": "{subject} is part of {object}",
        "isPartOf": "{subject} is part of {object}",
        "bird": "{subject}'s state bird is {object}",
        "leader": "{subject} is led by {object}",
        "affiliation": "{subject} is affiliated with {object}",
        "competeIn": "{subject} competes in {object}",
        "president": "{subject}'s president is {object}",
        "gemstone": "{subject}'s state gemstone is {object}",
        "mascot": "{subject}'s mascot is {object}",
        "cosparId": "{subject} has COSPAR ID {object}",
        "utcOffset": "{subject} has UTC offset {object}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject
    subject_groups = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_groups:
            subject_groups[t.subject] = []
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    # Find the main subject (prefer astronaut-related subjects)
    astronaut_keywords = ["mission", "backupPilot", "commander", "crewMembers", "operator", "cosparId"]
    main_subject = None
    for subject, group_triples in subject_groups.items():
        for t in group_triples:
            if t.predicate in astronaut_keywords:
                main_subject = subject
                break
        if main_subject:
            break

    if not main_subject:
        main_subject = list(subject_groups.keys())[0]

    # Build the sentence starting with main subject
    main_clauses = []
    other_subjects = []

    for subject, group_triples in subject_groups.items():
        for t in group_triples:
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                if subject == main_subject:
                    clause = tmpl.format(subject=subject, object=t.object)
                    main_clauses.append(clause)
                else:
                    # For other subjects, create relative clause connections
                    clause = tmpl.format(subject=subject, object=t.object)
                    other_subjects.append((subject, clause, t.predicate))
            else:
                # Create natural language for unknown predicates
                pred = t.predicate.replace("_", " ")
                if subject == main_subject:
                    # Use relative clause format for unknown predicates
                    clause = f"{pred} {t.object}"
                    main_clauses.append(clause)
                else:
                    clause = f"{pred} {t.object}"
                    other_subjects.append((subject, clause, t.predicate))

    # Combine all clauses into ONE complex sentence using relative clauses
    if not main_clauses:
        return ""

    # Start with the first clause about the main subject
    base_sentence = main_clauses[0]

    # Add remaining main subject clauses using relative pronouns for better flow
    for clause in main_clauses[1:]:
        # Determine the appropriate relative pronoun based on predicate type
        # Check which predicate this clause came from
        clause_predicate = None
        for t in triples:
            if t.subject == main_subject:
                tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
                if tmpl and tmpl.format(subject=main_subject, object=t.object) == clause:
                    clause_predicate = t.predicate
                    break

        # Use appropriate relative pronoun
        if clause_predicate in ["birthPlace", "deathPlace", "isPartOf", "part", "birthDate", "deathDate"]:
            if "in" in clause:
                base_sentence += f", where {clause.replace(main_subject + ' ', '')}"
            elif "on" in clause:
                base_sentence += f", on {clause.replace(main_subject + ' ', '')}"
            else:
                base_sentence += f", who {clause.replace(main_subject + ' ', '')}"
        else:
            base_sentence += f", and {clause.replace(main_subject + ' ', '')}"

    # Add information about other subjects using relative clauses
    if other_subjects:
        for subject, clause, predicate in other_subjects:
            # Determine connection type
            if predicate in ["birthPlace", "deathPlace", "isPartOf", "part"]:
                base_sentence += f", where {clause.replace(subject + ' ', '')}"
            elif predicate in ["operator", "commander", "backupPilot", "crewMembers", "mission"]:
                base_sentence += f", which {clause.replace(subject + ' ', '')}"
            elif predicate in ["affiliation", "competeIn"]:
                base_sentence += f", which is affiliated with {clause.replace(subject + ' is affiliated with ', '')}"
            else:
                base_sentence += f", which {clause.replace(subject + ' ', '')}"

    # Clean up the sentence - remove duplicate subject references
    import re
    # Remove repeated subject names after commas (keep first occurrence)
    pattern = rf", {re.escape(main_subject)}\b"
    base_sentence = re.sub(pattern, ",", base_sentence)

    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation
    sentence = base_sentence[0].upper() + base_sentence[1:]
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."

    # Remove any extra spaces before punctuation
    sentence = re.sub(r'\s+([.,])', r'\1', sentence)

    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
